pattern: A greedy single pass that fills each line until the next letter would overflow the fixed pixel budget, then starts a new line.
hints: Walk the string left to right keeping a running width for the current line.
Before placing a letter, check whether adding its width would exceed the line limit; if so, finish the line and begin a new one.
Track the number of lines opened and the width accumulated on the final line, returning both.

edge_inputs.description: uniform widths wrap the alphabet into three lines
a narrow first letter changes where the wrap lands
single character fits on one line
line fills to exactly the limit then the next letter starts a fresh line
maximum-width letters force many short lines
all letters fit comfortably on one line

stress.description: random widths and a random string to exercise varied wrap points
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import string
    widths = [rng.randint(2, 10) for _ in range(26)]
    length = rng.randint(1, max(1, size))
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(string.ascii_lowercase) for _ in range(length))
    return [widths, s]
stress.note: covers diverse letter widths and lengths
